headlines.title: What to Ask and What to Trust When Using AI for Medical Advice
headlines.description: A new study finds three in 10 adults use AI for health advice, so NBC chief technology analyst Joanna Stern and NBC medical correspondent Akshay Syal stop by TODAY to share how to most effectively use the technology. They share the importance of using AI to learn rather than diagnose, how to avoid sharing your personal information, and more.

headlines.title: FDA Advisory Committee to Review Peptide Restrictions Underway
headlines.description: Peptides are often being promoted by wellness influencers and podcasters as treatments for everything from injuries and aging and now an FDA panel is meeting to discuss whether to loosen restrictions and make them easier to get. But critics say there's not enough data on safety and efficacy. NBC’s medical contributor Natalie Azar joins TODAY to break down the seven peptides being discussed and how effective they are.

headlines.title: Regular Coffee Consumption Linked to Health Benefits, AHA Says
headlines.description: A new report from the American Heart Association says that it's safe for most adults to drink up to five cups of coffee per day. New research also suggests regular coffee consumption may be linked to lower risk of stroke, diabetes, and heart failure for some individuals. Health benefits apply to paper filtered or instant coffee.

headlines.title: Best Summer Nutrition Advice: Fiber Intake, Warm Water, More
headlines.description: Dr. Amy Shah, board-certified physician and nutritionist and New York Times bestselling author of “Hormone Havoc,” joins TODAY to break down summer nutrition trends including low-carb diets and intermittent fasting, protein foods and more.

headlines.title: Macy’s Black Friday in July sale — save up to 70% on bestselling brands
headlines.description: During the Macy’s Black Friday in July sale you can save on skin care, apparel, fragrance, appliances and more.

headlines.title: The best ways to avoid (and treat) heat rash and sun poisoning, according to derms
headlines.description: I talked to dermatologists about the best way to avoid sun-related conditions outside of the usual sunburn, including heat rash and sun poisoning, plus the best treatments for these concerns

headlines.title: How to watch the 2026 MLS season
headlines.description: This is everything you need to know about watching the 2026 MLS season, including streaming options and info on when the season takes place.

headlines.title: How often should you wash and change out your sheets? Experts explain
headlines.description: I talked to laundry experts to learn how often you should change and wash your bedding and when it’s time to throw them out for a new set.

headlines.title: RFK Jr. says cyclosporiasis outbreak is ‘under control’ amid probe into its origins
headlines.description: Mexico said its own investigation hasn’t found evidence linking the outbreak to the Taylor Farms facility in central Mexico.

headlines.title: Cyclospora parasite not found in any Taylor Farm lettuce samples after product recall, FDA says
headlines.description: A Food and Drug Administration test on a sample of lettuce from a supplier that has recalled products because of a multistate cyclosporiasis outbreak produced a false positive for the parasite, the agency said Sunday.

headlines.title: FDA says it’s still investigating Taylor Farms in cyclosporiasis outbreak
headlines.description: Taylor Farms continues to be the subject of a Food and Drug Administration investigation into the growing cyclosporiasis outbreak, despite a false positive test result, the agency said Monday.

headlines.title: We want to hear how Medicaid work requirements are affecting you
headlines.description: If you have lost your health coverage or are at risk of losing it, tell us your story.
